reviews.review_text: Fun game, but probably get Don't Starve Together instead, as that gets more content, even if you're playing solo.
A great little survival game that'll basically run on anything. Always have loved this game and found myself coming back to it a lot!
It's such a fun game. Really chill, until it isn't. but mostly chill.

Тhe best game, it destroyed my life but i still love it, i cant stop playing it, i just love dst and ds so much, give it a time and learn the things, it will be fun, dont worry

This is the game I've spent the most time playing so far. I will never forget the feeling of launching the game for the first time and seeing those evergreen trees swaying in the wind, even though they were just textures and animations. Most of the time, I just run around on the map, chopping trees, mining, collecting resources, and planning my survival schedule. For me, this is the most interesting part of the whole game, even more so than fighting bosses. I think it and DST are two separate games. The most reason I don't play DST is that I don't want to approach it with the mindset I've developed for playing Don't Starve. Although the DST has more content, it's too overwhelming for solo play. I feel like I'm seeing the game's logic exposed through a crack in the veil, so it suddenly becomes less interesting. Anyway, Don't Starve is a great game, and it's a shame that it's not getting any more content, but good things always come with a touch of regret, don't they?

Honestly really fun, but a pretty hard game. Though I've heard from others that this is one of those games that just gets easier the more you learn about it (which now that I've typed that out sounds stupid as I'm sure any game or anything in general gets easier the more you learn about it). I think this is really fun and the art style is really cool. I also like the character designs and how each of them have their own unique traits like how one person likes to set fire to things and another likes to lift heavy things, etc. I think ideas like that are always fun to play around with. Overall I think this game is worth taking a gander at.

A great game that is both harsh on players and fair. I love the storyline and it's a good challenge for people who like that kind of play style. Plenty of replayability and all of the characters are unique and have their own charm. The game in general had a unique and interesting atmosphere that I really enjoy. Definitely worth it.

yet another game i buy becouse i know i like it but then i never end up playing, i strongly recommend buying this game IF you intend of playing it longer then my dumbass.
(i bought it 16 Feb 2024 if anyone wanted to know)

Good game, fun game, mean game. Lots of options to customize how cruel the world is, and lots of mods to change what the normal options don't cover. I definitely didn't starve after 300 days because I got lost during winter.

The pure, lonely, and uncompromising roguelike masterpiece where you truly face the dark alone.

While Don't Starve Together has evolved into a massive, cooperative live-service giant, returning to the original single-player Don't Starve is a stark reminder of where this beautifully twisted universe began. Without friends to revive you, share the resource load, or keep watch at night, the game transforms into a deeply isolating, high-stakes psychological horror roguelike. The iconic, hand-drawn Gothic art style and scratchy jazz soundtrack feel even more oppressive when you are the sole target of the Constant. It is a masterpiece of pure survival design that strips away the safety net of multiplayer and forces you to rely entirely on your own wits, map knowledge, and adaptability.

What makes the standalone single-player game an absolute must-own—even if you already play DST—is its brilliant "Adventure Mode" and the incredible expansion trilogy: Reign of Giants, Shipwrecked, and Hamlet. Adventure Mode gives the game a definitive, narrative-driven endgame where you must survive increasingly brutal, themed worlds to dethrone Maxwell. Meanwhile, the DLCs completely reinvent the core loop. Shipwrecked trades the gloomy woods for a tropical archipelago filled with sailing mechanics, tidal waves, and volcano eruptions, while Hamlet drops you into an aristocratic society of sophisticated Pigmen where you navigate an actual economy, ancient ruins, and a looming Aporkalypse. The sheer variety across these single-player expansions offers a radically different and often more experimental flavor of survival than the multiplayer version.

Ultimately, the original Don't Starve stands tall as a flawless time capsule of Klei Entertainment's early genius. It doesn't feature the constant character reworks or the meta-heavy endgame raids of DST, but it offers something arguably better for solo players: a tight, pauseable, and beautifully paced challenge that values atmosphere and exploration above all else. Every mistake is a permanent lesson, and every successful winter survived feels like a personal triumph against a world that actively hates you. It is a legendary indie classic that remains the gold standard for solo survival on Steam.

10/10 — No teammates to blame when the dark takes you. Just you, your torch, and a very angry Deerclops.

This game is such a blast to play, its maybe in fact the best survivor games on this platform. The art style is amazing like a story book and it is easy to start getting a groove. The tuff part is living past winter for me, but it is still fun to keep trying.
used to play this years ago finally got it again recently alongside don't starve together great game
Great grinding game. Especially people who love calmness but action at the same time its a good mixture of both. not really a story game in my opinion.

I got this game because I heard great things about it and because of the collab. Little did I know this is not the actually enjoyable and interesting version, that being dont starve together. I've tried again and again to enjoy this game but I get punished for exploring and trying new things. Every guide i watch is about the other version and apparently 99% of the things in that game are missing from here. I feel it is incredibly dishonest to offer a worse experience to people who bought the actual game, not knowing the other is a sequel. DST was advertised as a multiplayer version of this game, not the only playable one. (yes i have a skill issue, but i still have beaten games that are considered very difficult, this one is purely frustrating

it may not be the best survival game but it deserves a positive review

To be honest, this game is exactly what I expected it to be. It is a game so punishing and confusing that it is only fun for the hardcore or the sadistic. Fortunately for me, I am neither of those people, but unfortunately that means I don't enjoy this game. It has charm, but only play it if you enjoy losing all of your progress to something you didn't understand.

I love this game so much! I originally found it on the mobile version and was addicted. Then my partner let me know it was a desktop game, and I never opened the app again. If nothing else, I will always value being able to carry one more thing in my hands when my pockets get full. I've since learned that my keyboard numbers are equivalent to my first few inventory slots! Okay, enough on mechanis. This is a first person survival game with perma death. I happen to be someone who sturggles to figure out mid-late game strategy, so the early game is my great love. It's a cute art style, and the sound design is fantastic. You can enjoy the basics like I do, but it also has so much hidden depth for those who want a challenge. It's one of those games that are hard, not because they are complicated, but because the conditions are tough. You need to plan ahead, always be looking out for Murphy's law, and most importantly: Don't Starve!
I got jumped by spiders twice, 8.5/10 would play again
very nice surviving game, don't be afraid to die!
